items.title: [负面] I like this game, a lot- I really do. Turn based g...
items.summary:
I like this game, a lot- I really do. Turn based games of this style itch me just right. Especially since they're so generous with gacha. In all honesty, I SHOULD be head over heels addicted to this game. Except the Difficulty spikes way too early.  I understand I have to strategize, but I'm barely mid way through the game and there are bosses who almost one shot my HP with shields mattering little to none. You might think I'm under leveled but they cap how strong you can make your heroes constantly, so I'm just trying to a build a deck that lets me kill bosses as soon as possible since trying to go about it with a balanced playstyle is pointless. In fact, there were times I lost simply due to bad RNG with the death resistance because the boss repeatedly triggers it over and over. I mean, I have 2 strong healers on my team yet their combined efforts hardly gives me 150 HP while the boss can hit me for 500+?? Its insane. 

Aside from that though, I can't comment on everything else the negative reviews are leaving. So if the difficulty of certain bosses get nerfed, I'll be able to change my review to positive.

4-15-2026 Edit: Okay so now that I've played a lot more I can leave a more accurate review. Keep in mind I play this on phone and PC, so the hours of how long I've played this game are inaccurate.

I have gotten better units, made them much stronger, etc etc. However, what I said about the power creep is still true. This game suffers from a phenomenon that I don't think has an official name so I'm going to call "Hypothetical Power Creep". Essentially, this game is very generous with summons and summoning currency, and the rarity of SSRs isn't that low. That means, hypothetically, that getting 5 stars is easy. Which, it is. However, its for that reason that the game heavily ramps up in difficulty. Here's the thing-- when you get multiple of a unit, you can ascend them with that dupe in order to enhance their existing skill set. Unlike most gacha games where the max you can ascend an existing unit is about 5, this game allows you to ascend an existing unit like 15 times. If you get 7 dupes, you'll unlock something called "Over Exalt", which is their ultimate skill but better. Every upgrade following 7 just improves stats. Now, BECAUSE the game is so generous and over exalt is super good, the game assumes you will repeatedly get the same unit and get over exalt early in the game. THIS is why the game creeps the way it does. It assumes you will be lucky with your abundance of resources and be extremely overpowered if the enemies aren't insanely difficult. 

However, as you can imagine-- this assumption creates problems. For starters, most players won't know who to focus on summoning on or building. The chance of you repeatedly summoning for someone intentionally is just low. Secondly, even with their generosity, its still RNG at the end of the day. Meaning you can use your saved up currency, not get enough for over exalt, and you'll still struggle early game without spending money. Thirdly, this is the type of game where you want to make teams around maximizing a character's usage. In short, you don't want to spend all your resources on one person. There's more issues of course, but you get the idea. 

Something else I REALLY don't like about the game is that a lot of their good units are ones who thrive in longer battles, like the banner that was just finished, Vortice. Her whole gimmick is that she does high pursuit damage against bosses when other allies do Exalt, and her Exalt is a nuke to the boss. If you're patient enough, you can up this damage to 100k+. Again, this on its own isn't a problem. The problem is that the game punishes you in so many ways if the battle goes on too long. Not only do a lot of the stage achievements want you to beat bosses INSANELY quickly (10-15 turns) despite them having 200k HP and 5 healthbars of the same amount half the time, but the bosses ramp up in difficulty by buffing themselves with attack up, damage taken decrease, and more?? It feels like the developers made it difficult for the sake of being difficult. It just doesn't feel playable sometimes. Which, they know-- which is why they added a revive feature that holds up to 5 charges and you get a new charge every day. You might think this annexes the problem, but no. For two reasons. One: On hard mode, the game wants you to beat the stages WITHOUT revives. Two: This gives the devs a reason to avoid fixing the power creep. So no matter what, you are punished in some form of way if you're not much stronger than the stage asks you to be.

This game also has questionable performance problems. For starters, just doing single player battle has a ping, which is usually at 200ms. So you're often lagging or experiencing delay in story mode. But that's doable for me. The deal breaker is that the game has a habit of freezing, lagging, or crashing on both my phone and computer which makes no sense to me considering they can handle heavier games without issue. 

Now, with all that said-- would I say don't play the game? No, I still think its worth playing, else I would've uninstalled. However I will say to play patiently and to do extra content to get stronger faster and not just story if you want to reduce the chance of you pulling your hair out because the boss is about to do 1.1k damage to you for some ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ reason.
